problems.title: Two Sum
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

Return the answer as an array of two integers.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers
- Third line: `target`

**Output format**
- Two indices `i j` (0-based) separated by a space.

The order does not matter.

problems.title: Contains Duplicate
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums`, return `true` if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return `false` if every element is distinct.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- `true` or `false`

problems.title: Maximum Subarray
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums`, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum and return its sum.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- Maximum subarray sum (integer)

problems.title: Valid Anagram
problems.statement_md:
Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `t` is an anagram of `s`, and `false` otherwise.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `s`
- Line 2: `t`

**Output format**
- `true` or `false`

problems.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the total number of continuous subarrays whose sum equals `k`.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers
- Third line: `k`

**Output format**
- Count of subarrays (integer)

problems.title: Move Zeroes
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums`, move all `0` values to the end of it while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements.

You must do this **in-place**.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- The resulting array as `n` space-separated integers

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.statement_md:
You are given an array `prices` where `prices[i]` is the price of a stock on day `i`.

Return the maximum profit you can achieve from a single buy and a single sell. If you cannot achieve any profit, return `0`.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- Maximum profit (integer)

problems.title: Product of Array Except Self
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums` of length `n` (where `n >= 2`), return an array `answer` such that `answer[i]` is the product of all the elements of `nums` except `nums[i]`.

You must write an algorithm that runs in `O(n)` time.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- `n` space-separated integers representing `answer`

problems.title: Intersection of Two Arrays II
problems.statement_md:
Given two integer arrays `nums1` and `nums2`, return an array of their intersection. Each element in the result must appear as many times as it shows in both arrays.

The output will be judged after sorting.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers (`nums1`)
- Third line: `m`
- Fourth line: `m` space-separated integers (`nums2`)

**Output format**
- The intersection elements as space-separated integers (may be empty)

problems.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
problems.statement_md:
Given an unsorted array of integers `nums`, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence.

You must write an algorithm that runs in `O(n)` time.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- Length of the longest consecutive sequence (integer)

problems.title: Ransom Note
problems.statement_md:
Given two strings `ransomNote` and `magazine`, return `true` if `ransomNote` can be constructed by using the letters from `magazine` and `false` otherwise.

Each letter in `magazine` can only be used once.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `ransomNote`
- Line 2: `magazine`

**Output format**
- `true` or `false`

problems.title: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements.

The output will be judged after sorting by frequency descending, and by value ascending for ties.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers
- Third line: `k`

**Output format**
- `k` integers separated by spaces

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.statement_md:
Given a string `s` containing only the characters `(`, `)`, `{`, `}`, `[` and `]`, return `true` if the input string is valid, and `false` otherwise.

A string is valid if:
- Open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets.
- Open brackets are closed in the correct order.
- Every close bracket has a corresponding open bracket.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `s`

**Output format**
- `true` or `false`

problems.title: Binary Search
problems.statement_md:
Given a sorted integer array `nums` and an integer `target`, return the index of `target` in `nums`. If `target` does not exist, return `-1`.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers (sorted)
- Third line: `target`

**Output format**
- Index of `target` or `-1`

problems.title: Reverse String
problems.statement_md:
Given a string `s`, return the string reversed.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `s`

**Output format**
- The reversed string

problems.title: Palindrome Number
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer `x`, return `true` if `x` is a palindrome, and `false` otherwise.

An integer is a palindrome when it reads the same backward as forward.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `x`

**Output format**
- `true` or `false`

problems.title: Climbing Stairs
problems.statement_md:
You are climbing a staircase. It takes `n` steps to reach the top.

Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?

**Input format**
- Line 1: `n`

**Output format**
- Number of distinct ways

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
problems.statement_md:
Given two sorted integer arrays `a` and `b`, return a new sorted array that contains all elements of `a` and `b`.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers (sorted)

- Third line: `m`
- Fourth line: `m` space-separated integers (sorted)

(If `n` or `m` is 0, the corresponding line will be empty.)

**Output format**
- `n + m` integers separated by spaces

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.statement_md:
Given a string `s`, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `s`

**Output format**
- Length of the longest substring without repeating characters

problems.title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
problems.statement_md:
You are given an array of tokens that represents an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation.

Return the integer result of the expression.

Valid operators are `+`, `-`, `*`, and `/`.

Note:
- Division between two integers should truncate toward zero.

**Input format**
- First line: `n` (number of tokens)
- Second line: `n` tokens separated by spaces

**Output format**
- The integer result

problems.title: Number of Islands
problems.statement_md:
Given an `r × c` grid of characters where `'1'` represents land and `'0'` represents water, return the number of islands.

An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.

**Input format**
- First line: `r c`
- Next `r` lines: a string of length `c` containing only `0` and `1`

**Output format**
- Number of islands

problems.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
problems.statement_md:
Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k`th largest element in the array.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers
- Third line: `k`

**Output format**
- The `k`th largest element

problems.title: Coin Change
problems.statement_md:
You are given an integer array `coins` representing coins of different denominations and an integer `amount`.

Return the fewest number of coins that you need to make up that amount. If that amount cannot be made up by any combination of the coins, return `-1`.

You may assume you have an infinite number of each kind of coin.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers (coin denominations)
- Third line: `amount`

**Output format**
- Minimum number of coins, or `-1`

problems.title: Merge Intervals
problems.statement_md:
Given an array of intervals where `intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i]`, merge all overlapping intervals and return the merged intervals.

**Input format**
- First line: `m` (number of intervals)
- Next `m` lines: `start end`

**Output format**
- First line: `k` (number of merged intervals)
- Next `k` lines: `start end`

problems.title: Course Schedule
problems.statement_md:
There are `n` courses labeled from `0` to `n - 1`.

You are given prerequisites where each pair `a b` indicates that to take course `a` you must first take course `b`.

Return `true` if you can finish all courses, otherwise return `false`.

**Input format**
- First line: `n m`
- Next `m` lines: `a b`

**Output format**
- `true` or `false`

problems.title: Minimum Window Substring
problems.statement_md:
Given two strings `s` and `t`, return the minimum window substring of `s` such that every character in `t` (including duplicates) is included in the window.

If there is no such substring, print `EMPTY`.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `s`
- Line 2: `t`

**Output format**
- The minimum window substring, or `EMPTY`

problems.title: Trapping Rain Water
problems.statement_md:
Given `n` non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- Total trapped water

problems.title: Word Ladder
problems.statement_md:
A transformation sequence from word `beginWord` to word `endWord` using a dictionary `wordList` is a sequence of words such that:
- The first word in the sequence is `beginWord`.
- The last word in the sequence is `endWord`.
- Only one letter can be changed at a time.
- Each transformed word must exist in `wordList`.

Return the length of the shortest transformation sequence, or 0 if no such sequence exists.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `beginWord`
- Line 2: `endWord`
- Line 3: `n` (number of words in the list)
- Next `n` lines: one word per line

**Output format**
- Shortest transformation length, or 0

problems.title: Longest Valid Parentheses
problems.statement_md:
Given a string `s` containing only `(` and `)`, return the length of the longest valid (well-formed) parentheses substring.

**Input format**
- Line 1: `s`

**Output format**
- Length of the longest valid parentheses substring

problems.title: Daily Temperatures
problems.statement_md:
Given a list of daily temperatures `temperatures`, return an array `answer` such that `answer[i]` is the number of days you have to wait after the `i`th day to get a warmer temperature.

If there is no future day for which this is possible, keep `answer[i] = 0`.

**Input format**
- First line: `n`
- Second line: `n` space-separated integers

**Output format**
- `n` integers separated by spaces
